title: lock down staging signups to github id allowlist
author: ElliotEtag
pr_body_excerpt:
Closes #11137.

`staging.enter.pollinations.ai` was publicly accessible — anyone could sign in via GitHub OAuth, mint an API key, and hit `staging.gen.pollinations.ai` which holds the **production** provider keys. Sandbox Stripe = fake money in, real OpenAI/Google/etc. bills out.

This gates GitHub OAuth sign-in on staging to an env-var allowlist of numeric GitHub IDs (`STAGING_ALLOWED_GITHUB_IDS`). Two Better Auth `databaseHooks`:
- `user.create.before` blocks new signups whose `githubId` isn't in the set
- `session.create.before` blocks every login (covers users that already exist in staging D1)

Both throw `APIError("FORBIDDEN")`, which Better Auth redirects to `/error?error=staging_is_invite-only`. Plugin is a no-op outside staging.

GitHub **IDs**, not usernames — usernames are mutable (silent lockout if someone renames). Not the DB user ID either — that only exists post-signup, so it can't gate a fresh OAuth callback.

Existing staging D1 already cleaned: 4 external dev accounts deleted + cascades. Remaining 5 users match the allowlist.

Initial allowlist:
- `36901823` ElliotEtag
- `5099901` voodoohop
- `235942848` thomashmyceli
- `248917639` fouchyelliot-rgb
- `241978997` pollen-router

Production untouched.

gist.headline: VIPs only in the staging garden
gist.blurb: We've locked down the staging environment to an allowlist of GitHub IDs. No more accidental free rides on the staging API!
gist.summary: Secured the staging environment by restricting GitHub OAuth sign-ins to a specific allowlist of GitHub IDs via Better Auth hooks.
gist.impact: Internal developers can still access staging, but public users are now blocked from minting API keys in the sandbox environment.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee acting as a friendly bouncer at a garden gate, checking a list. Represents the new allowlist for the staging environment.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of Polly the bee mascot wearing a tiny security badge, holding a clipboard and checking IDs at a glowing lime green (#ecf874) garden gate. A chunky retro robot waits in line. Soft pastel gradient background, warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Emotionally warm, nostalgic but beautiful.
